sourceMovie.title: Mank
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Mank (2020) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ed Wood
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mank clicked for you, Ed Wood (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: October Sky
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mank clicked for you, October Sky (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Mank?
faq.answer: Try Ed Wood, October Sky, The Current War—each shares DNA with Mank while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Mank?
faq.answer: Mank (2020) is best described as Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Mank worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
